scholarships.name: Holland Scholarship (NL Scholarship)
scholarships.provider: Dutch Ministry of Education, Culture and Science with participating Dutch institutions (administered by Nuffic)
scholarships.hostUniversities: Participating Dutch research universities and universities of applied sciences (e.g. Utrecht, Maastricht, VU Amsterdam, Wageningen, Eindhoven)
scholarships.fundingSummary: A one-off grant of €5,000 paid in the first year of study; not a full-tuition scholarship.
benefits: One-off grant of €5,000 in the first year
Helps offset tuition or living costs
Open to a wide range of bachelor's and master's programmes
Awarded across many participating Dutch institutions
No repayment required
Supports non-EEA international students studying in the Netherlands
eligibility: Non-EEA (non-EU/EEA) nationality
Applying to or enrolling in a participating bachelor's or master's programme
No prior degree or enrolment at a Dutch education institution
Meet the specific requirements of the chosen institution
Enrolling as a full-time student
Not eligible for statutory (lower) Dutch tuition fees
scholarships.deadlineNote: Deadlines vary by institution; commonly 1 February (some set 1 May). Check the chosen institution.
thingsToHaveBeforeApply: Admission application to a participating programme

scholarships.name: Orange Tulip Scholarship (OTS)
scholarships.hostUniversities: Participating Dutch universities and universities of applied sciences
scholarships.fundingSummary: Merit-based awards covering partial to full tuition fees, with coverage and amounts varying by university and country programme.
benefits: Tuition fee reduction ranging from partial up to full (100%) depending on the package
Wide choice of participating Dutch institutions and programmes
Scholarship packages tailored by each individual university
Open to Bachelor's and Master's applicants
Aimed at talented students from selected Neso countries
Administered by Nuffic through its Neso country offices
eligibility: National of an eligible Neso country (e.g. Brazil, China, India, Indonesia, Mexico, South Africa, South Korea, Vietnam)
Do not hold a degree from a Dutch educational institution
Meet the admission requirements of the chosen Dutch programme
Have applied or been admitted to a participating Dutch institution/OTS package
Demonstrate academic excellence and talent
documentsRequired: Proof of admission or application to a participating Dutch programme
scholarships.deadlineNote: Deadlines vary by country programme and university, typically February–May.
thingsToHaveBeforeApply: Admission to or application at a participating Dutch programme
Valid passport / proof of eligible nationality

scholarships.name: Chinese Government Scholarship (CSC)
scholarships.provider: China Scholarship Council (CSC), Ministry of Education of China
scholarships.fundingSummary: Full scholarship covering tuition, campus accommodation, monthly living allowance and comprehensive medical insurance.
benefits: Full tuition waiver
Free on-campus accommodation or housing allowance
Monthly living allowance: Bachelors CNY 2,500, Masters CNY 3,000, PhD CNY 3,500
Comprehensive medical insurance for international students in China
Exemption from application/registration fees
Chinese language study support where required
eligibility: Non-Chinese citizen in good health
Bachelors: high school graduate, generally under 25
Masters: hold a bachelor's degree, generally under 35
PhD: hold a master's degree, generally under 40
Not concurrently enrolled at a Chinese university
Meet the admission requirements of the host university and program
documentsRequired: Chinese Government Scholarship application form (completed in online system)
Notarized highest diploma/certificate of expected graduation
Study plan or research proposal in Chinese or English
Language proficiency certificate (HSK or English) if applicable
scholarships.deadlineNote: Varies by agency/embassy/university, typically January–April; confirm with your channel
scholarships.durationNote: Bachelors 4-5 yrs; Masters 2-3 yrs; PhD 3-4 yrs (plus 1-2 yr Chinese language if required)
thingsToHaveBeforeApply: Agency number of your application channel (embassy/university)
Pre-admission or acceptance letter from a Chinese university (recommended)
Academic transcripts and diploma ready to upload
Study/research plan and referees for recommendation letters

scholarships.name: Romanian Government Scholarship
scholarships.provider: Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Romania
fields: All fields except Medicine, Dental Medicine and Pharmacy
scholarships.fundingSummary: Covers tuition, a monthly stipend and dormitory accommodation, plus a Romanian language preparatory year.
benefits: Full tuition exemption for the study cycle
Monthly stipend for the duration of studies
Free tuition for the Romanian language preparatory year
Dormitory accommodation subject to availability
Exemption from registration and application fees
Study at accredited Romanian state universities
eligibility: Non-EU citizen (not from EU member states)
Not of Romanian origin or from Romanian historical communities
Minimum study-years average of at least 7.0 (or 'Good' equivalent)
Not already a beneficiary of a Romanian state scholarship for the same cycle
Not holding protection/stateless status recognized in Romania
Submit a complete application file with recognized education documents
documentsRequired: Letter of intent and research project (PhD applicants)
Apostilled/authenticated translations in Romanian, English, French or Spanish
scholarships.deadlineNote: Applications typically open mid-February and close end of March; results around mid-July.
scholarships.durationNote: Includes a Romanian language preparatory year for non-speakers
thingsToHaveBeforeApply: Passport valid for the study period

scholarships.name: Mastercard Foundation Scholars Program
scholarships.hostUniversities: University of Cape Town, Makerere University, Ashesi University, University of Pretoria, Arizona State University, McGill University, University of Toronto, University of Edinburgh, American University of Beirut (62 partner institutions)
scholarships.fundingSummary: Comprehensive support covering tuition, accommodation, living costs, and holistic academic and leadership development.
benefits: Full tuition fees
Accommodation
Books and academic materials
Living expenses/stipend
Mentoring and counseling (psychosocial support)
Leadership development and transition support
Return airfare where necessary
eligibility: Primarily African youth and those from underserved communities
Economically disadvantaged with financial need
Academically talented and high-achieving
Service-oriented with leadership potential and commitment to giving back
Meets the admission requirements of the chosen partner institution
Specific criteria set by each individual partner institution
documentsRequired: Proof of admission or application to a partner institution
scholarships.deadlineNote: Each partner institution sets its own deadlines and requirements; apply directly through the chosen partner.
thingsToHaveBeforeApply: Shortlist of Scholars Program partner institutions and their deadlines

scholarships.name: KAIST International Undergraduate Scholarship
scholarships.provider: Korea Advanced Institute of Science and Technology (KAIST)
scholarships.fundingSummary: Admitted international undergraduates receive full tuition exemption for 8 semesters plus a monthly living allowance and health insurance.
benefits: Full tuition exemption for 8 semesters (up to 4 years)
Monthly living allowance of 350,000 KRW
Medical/health insurance coverage
Automatic consideration on admission (no separate scholarship application)
Renewed by maintaining a GPA above 2.7/4.3 after the freshman year
English-taught degree programs with a non-declared major (free major exploration)
eligibility: Applicant must hold foreign (non-Korean) citizenship; Korean or dual citizens are ineligible
Neither of the applicant's parents is a Korean citizen (Korean-origin applicants must have completed all schooling abroad)
Completed or expected high school graduation (12 years of primary/secondary education)
Must apply as a freshman; transfer students are not accepted
International schools located in Korea are not recognized as foreign schools
Must not hold a degree from another university upon enrollment
documentsRequired: One recommendation letter from a teacher
Copy of passport or national ID
Standardized test score (SAT, ACT, AP, IB, or GCE A-Level)
English proficiency test score (TOEFL, IELTS, TEPS, or TOEIC)
Optional: high school profile and up to five honors/awards
scholarships.applicationFee: USD 80 (fee waiver for top-ranked applicants from UN Least Developed Countries)
scholarships.deadlineNote: Two rounds: Early Admission (Spring track closes late Oct, Fall track mid-Jan) and Regular Admission (Fall, closes mid-January).
thingsToHaveBeforeApply: A teacher willing to submit a recommendation letter
Copy of passport or national ID

scholarships.name: Bocconi Merit Award / Bocconi International Awards
scholarships.fundingSummary: A 100% waiver on Bocconi's ordinary academic tuition and fees for top-ranked first-year Bachelor and Master students.
benefits: 100% waiver on Bocconi ordinary academic tuition and fees
Awarded automatically on the strength of the admission dossier
No separate scholarship application required
Assignment communicated together with admission results
Open to international, Italian and Bocconi applicants
Renewable for the full duration of the degree if merit is maintained
eligibility: Admitted first-year Bachelor or Master of Science student for a.y. 2026-27
Academic profile ranked among the top admitted applicants
Assessed on the merit of the submitted admission dossier
Open to international, Italian and Bocconi students
No separate award application needed — considered automatically at admission
documentsRequired: Updated CV (to be uploaded upon acceptance)
scholarships.deadlineNote: No separate award deadline; assessed automatically at admission, so apply in an early admission round.
scholarships.durationNote: Renewable for degree duration if enrollment deadlines are met and required credits earned
thingsToHaveBeforeApply: Required admission test results (Bocconi test, SAT, GMAT or GRE as applicable)

scholarships.name: University of Twente Scholarship (UTS)
scholarships.fundingSummary: Partial scholarship of €3,000–€22,000 per year as compensation for study-related costs for excellent non-EU/EEA Master's students.
benefits: Award ranging from €3,000 to €22,000 for one year
Compensation for study-related costs, spent at the student's discretion
Possible continuation into a second year for two-year Master's programmes
Applicable to 30+ qualifying UT Master's programmes
Supports talented non-EU/EEA international students
eligibility: Non-EU/EEA (international) student
(Provisionally) admitted to a qualifying UT Master's programme starting September
Has a UT student number
Among the best 5–10% of your graduation class (excellent students)
Academic IELTS 6.5 (or TOEFL iBT 90), with 6.0 (TOEFL iBT 20) on speaking
Not eligible for a Dutch study loan
Has not graduated from a UT (under)graduate programme
documentsRequired: Proof of (provisional) admission to a qualifying Master's programme
Completed online UTS scholarship application form
scholarships.deadlineNote: One annual round; applications open around mid-October and close 1 April (16:00 CET) for the September start.
scholarships.durationNote: One year, extendable to two years for two-year Master's programmes on meeting performance requirements
thingsToHaveBeforeApply: (Provisional) admission to a qualifying Master's programme
